faqs.question: How long does a water heater typically last?
faqs.answer: A standard tank water heater typically lasts 8–12 years, while a tankless water heater can last 15–20 years with proper maintenance. Regular flushing and inspections help extend its lifespan.

faqs.question: Should I switch to a tankless water heater?
faqs.answer: Tankless water heaters are more energy-efficient and provide endless hot water on demand. They're a great option for homes with high hot water usage. We can help you evaluate whether a tankless system is right for your home.

faqs.question: What are signs my water heater needs to be replaced?
faqs.answer: Common signs include rusty or discolored water, strange noises, inconsistent water temperature, leaks around the base, and the unit being over 10 years old. Contact us for a professional assessment.

faqs.question: Do you offer emergency water heater repair?
faqs.answer: Yes. We provide 24/7 emergency water heater repair services. If you're without hot water, call us at {phone} and we'll get a technician to your home as soon as possible.
